title: racosperma congestum subsp. wonganense
intro: Racosperma congestum subsp. wonganense, also known as Wongan pea, is a species of legume in the family Fabaceae, native to Western Australia. It has been traditionally used in Western Australian Aboriginal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues. The plant has been reported to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in wound healing. Racosperma congestum subsp. wonganense can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and topical applications, depending on the intended use.
